methods.description: This method describes the process of analyzing sequence data to identify and classify bacterial communities from samples.

steps.description: Forward and reverse reads were joined with fastq-join and accepted only if longer than 250 bp.
steps.searchable_summary: join forward and reverse reads using fastq-join, filter by length > 250 bp

steps.description: Quality filtering (Emax = 1, no ambiguous characters), dereplication (including filtering of singletons), denoising (including chimera removal), and clustering at 97% identity were performed with USEARCH v10.
steps.searchable_summary: perform quality filtering, dereplication, denoising, and clustering using USEARCH v10

steps.description: Assigned taxonomies using the RDP v16 reference database using the same software.
steps.searchable_summary: assign taxonomies using RDP v16 database

steps.description: The dataset was further analyzed in R 3.2.4 using the packages phyloseq, ggplot2, microbiome, MASS, and vegan.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze dataset in R using phyloseq, ggplot2, microbiome, MASS, and vegan

steps.description: Taxa assigned as chloroplasts, mitochondria, or unclassified were removed.
steps.searchable_summary: remove chloroplast, mitochondria, and unclassified taxa

steps.description: Computed the Shannon diversity for all samples, and an ANOVA test was conducted to compare these between healthy and deceased larvae.
steps.searchable_summary: compute Shannon diversity and perform ANOVA test

steps.description: Data were relativized, and differences in community composition were visualized with Bray-Curtis-based non-metric multidimensional scaling (NMDS).
steps.searchable_summary: visualize community composition using NMDS

steps.description: Bacterial communities between larvae and respective community distance matrices for larvae and respective pollen provisions were compared with Mantel tests based on Pearson's correlation.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Mantel tests to compare bacterial communities

steps.description: Permutational multivariate analysis of variance (PERMANOVA) and betadisper were used to assess overlap and variability between and within sample groups.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PERMANOVA and betadisper analysis

steps.description: To find co-occurrences between bacterial taxa in deceased larvae, we used the program SparCC for pairwise correlations with 1000 bootstrap replicates to calculate pseudo p-values.
steps.searchable_summary: use SparCC for pairwise correlations and calculate pseudo p-values

steps.description: Performed a linear discriminant (LD) analysis with treatment type set as the discriminative class.
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear discriminant analysis with treatment type

methods.description: This method outlines the experimental setup for collecting and processing samples from artificial trap nests to study the bacterial communities in O. bicornis larvae.

steps.description: Sampling took place in northern Bavaria, Germany. Artificial trap nests were established on the sites in April 2016 and thereafter checked regularly.
steps.searchable_summary: establish and monitor artificial trap nests in northern Bavaria

steps.description: Occupied nests were collected in the time frame from 25 May 2016 to 30 June 2016.
steps.searchable_summary: collect occupied nests between May and June 2016

steps.description: Nests were opened horizontally in order to not disrupt the nest chambers.
steps.searchable_summary: open nests horizontally to preserve chambers

steps.description: Removed the rearmost deceased female larva, its respective remaining pollen provision, and the soil separator from each nest.
steps.searchable_summary: remove rearmost deceased female larva and associated materials

steps.description: Transferred samples into sterile 1.5-mL tubes and stored at −25°C until further processing.
steps.searchable_summary: store samples in sterile tubes at −25°C

steps.description: Divided 31 larvae into three groups for different treatments in a microbiological safety cabinet.
steps.searchable_summary: divide larvae into treatment groups

steps.description: Group A larvae were placed on untreated pollen clumps.
steps.searchable_summary: place group A larvae on untreated pollen

steps.description: Group B larvae were placed on sterilized pollen clumps.
steps.searchable_summary: place group B larvae on sterilized pollen

steps.description: Group C larvae were placed on pollen provisions inoculated with a Bacillus sp. strain.
steps.searchable_summary: place group C larvae on Bacillus-inoculated pollen

steps.description: After feeding for five days, larvae were transferred into sterile 1.5-mL tubes and stored at −25°C.
steps.searchable_summary: store fed larvae in sterile tubes at −25°C
